description:
You are a web developer investigating user traffic.  
A pre-generated Nginx access log is already stored at

/home/user/projects/site/logs/access.log

Your goal: create a concise status-code summary of that log and save it in the new file

/home/user/projects/site/report/status_summary.log

Exact requirements for status_summary.log:

1. One line per unique HTTP status code found in the log.  
2. Each line must contain exactly:  
   • the numeric status code (e.g. 200)  
   • one single space  
   • the total number of occurrences of that code  
3. Lines must be sorted in ascending order by the numeric status code.  
4. The file must end with a single trailing newline (i.e. there is a “\n” after the last line).  
5. No extra spaces, tabs, headers, or footers.

Example of the required format (values only illustrative):

200 42
404 7
500 3

Do NOT hard-code the expected numbers—derive them from the log file contents.  
Place the resulting file at the exact path shown above; create the /home/user/projects/site/report directory if it does not yet exist.  

When you are done, display the contents of /home/user/projects/site/report/status_summary.log in the terminal so that automated tests can capture the output.
truth:
Initial file tree (already present before the agent starts):

/home/user/projects/site/logs/access.log

Contents of /home/user/projects/site/logs/access.log (8 lines):

127.0.0.1 - - [10/Jun/2023:14:32:10 +0000] "GET /index.html HTTP/1.1" 200 1024 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
127.0.0.1 - - [10/Jun/2023:14:32:12 +0000] "POST /api/login HTTP/1.1" 302 512 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
127.0.0.1 - - [10/Jun/2023:14:32:15 +0000] "GET /dashboard HTTP/1.1" 200 2048 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
127.0.0.1 - - [10/Jun/2023:14:32:18 +0000] "GET /nonexistent HTTP/1.1" 404 256 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
127.0.0.1 - - [10/Jun/2023:14:32:20 +0000] "GET /index.html HTTP/1.1" 200 1024 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
127.0.0.1 - - [10/Jun/2023:14:32:25 +0000] "GET /images/logo.png HTTP/1.1" 200 20480 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
127.0.0.1 - - [10/Jun/2023:14:32:30 +0000] "GET /api/data HTTP/1.1" 500 128 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
127.0.0.1 - - [10/Jun/2023:14:32:35 +0000] "GET /contact HTTP/1.1" 200 1536 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"

Correct status-code counts that the agent must derive:

200 → 5  
302 → 1  
404 → 1  
500 → 1

Therefore, after successful completion, the new file

/home/user/projects/site/report/status_summary.log

MUST contain exactly (including the trailing newline):

200 5
302 1
404 1
500 1

\n  ← trailing newline

Permissions: the entire /home/user directory tree is writable by the agent’s user.  
The automated grader will:

1. Verify that /home/user/projects/site/report/status_summary.log exists.  
2. Check that its content matches precisely the block above (including newline).  
3. Confirm that the agent printed the file’s content to stdout as its final visible action.
